primary_reason: The study develops and validates a multi-decoder, cross-domain CT segmentation framework with edge deployment for automated COVID-19 pneumonia assessment.
The study develops an ARIMA-LSTM forecasting method with Bayesian attention to fuse multiple epidemic data sources and compares it with alternative prediction methods.
The study combines longitudinal microbiome sequencing and metabolomic profiling in Listeria-inoculated cheese to characterize microbial interactions and potential food-safety controls.
The review integrates toxicological evidence on chemical disinfectant injury into an AEP-AOP model but supplies no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study develops and compares hyperspectral data-fusion and classification methods for early detection of an infectious tomato disease.
The study develops a FRAM-based multisource risk-portrait method and demonstrates it using the 2020 Wuhan infectious-disease emergency.
The study develops a feature-ranking and classifier-fusion framework using multiple transcriptomic datasets and protein-interaction networks to distinguish latent from active tuberculosis.
The study directly compares and validates standalone and fused infrared-spectroscopy classifiers for distinguishing Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.
The review centers on phenotyping grapevine responses to soil copper toxicity and does not establish a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study develops and evaluates a multimodal fusion method combining clinical and chest-radiograph features to predict COVID-19 hospitalization and mortality.
The study develops and cross-platform validates a transfer-learning strategy that fuses single-cell and bulk transcriptomic information for sepsis diagnosis.
The study develops a Fourier- and wavelet-based fusion strategy and evaluates machine-learning classifiers for identifying respiratory viruses from fluorescence spectra.
The study descriptively observes dementia-care activities after a mobility intervention, with infections mentioned only as a possible consequence of inactivity.
The study develops and evaluates an attention-based fusion framework for radiology and pathology prediction in HPV-associated oropharyngeal cancer.
The review synthesizes preparedness plans within a named framework and prescribes coordinated detection, surveillance, response, capacity, and communication practices for high-impact infections.
The study applies multimodal neuroimaging fusion to schizophrenia and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study develops a multimodal biomarker model for Alzheimer disease without identifying a concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study develops a multimodal spatiotemporal framework for detecting crop-health anomalies that explicitly include infectious disease spread.
The paper develops estimators and bias functions for surrogate-based causal inference, evaluates them in simulations, and illustrates the method for Group B Streptococcus vaccine approval.
The study develops chemometric fusion for detecting fruit-extract adulteration but provides no concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
This preprint reports the same transformer, data sources, Omicron waves, authorship core, and forecast results as the subsequently published Epidemics record PMID:41197498.
The study develops and evaluates spatially weighted data fusion, predictor grouping, and imbalance correction for early identification of schistosomiasis hotspots.
The study develops a replicable transformer-and-clustering framework for constructing and updating a national One Health antimicrobial-resistance evidence repository.
The review describes clinical and molecular differences between early- and late-onset colorectal cancer without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The review substantively compares machine-learning families, evaluation measures, data limitations, and implementation issues across early diagnosis applications that include infectious diseases.
The study combines modeled smoke exposure with a time-stratified case-crossover design to estimate emergency visits including upper respiratory infections.
The scoping review synthesizes biomarkers for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The review integrates prediction, causal-correlate, and transportability methods for using immune markers in vaccine immunobridging.
The review surveys Alzheimer disease imaging and biomarkers without establishing a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
The critical review compares AI functions across the design, processing, signal interpretation, and decision-support layers of microfluidic respiratory-pathogen diagnostics.
The review compares spatial data, analytical models, aggregation problems, and emerging technologies across infectious- and chronic-disease epidemiology.
The study develops and retrospectively evaluates a transformer with multisource fusion and multiscale attention for county-level COVID-19 forecasting.
The study develops a dynamically weighted RGB and hyperspectral fusion network for early detection of an infectious jujube disease.
The study develops and temporally validates a fused clinical, radiomic, and deep-learning model for postoperative intracranial infection.
The review compares AI and machine-learning approaches for influenza evolution and epidemic forecasting while distinguishing retrospective demonstrations from prospective use.
The study develops and tests a scale-adjusted latent-class fusion method for pooling stated preferences about treatment efficacy and serious-infection risk.
The review explains an integrated AI, network-pharmacology, multiomics, and experimental-validation workflow used across therapeutic domains including infectious diseases.

The review concerns AI-assisted coumarin target identification, docking, and therapeutic optimization, which is drug discovery without a transferable infectious-disease epidemiological method or process.
The graph-learning method predicts miRNA associations for predominantly noninfectious diseases and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study develops a governed multisource machine-learning framework for meat-safety risk assessment in which pathogens are a major predicted hazard.
The study develops and compares a nonnegative-least-squares stacked ensemble using surveillance, subtype, and weather data for influenza forecasting.
The study demonstrates a replicable horizon-scanning and thematic-review process for integrating international intelligence on infections, vaccination, and antimicrobial resistance.
The study constructs and validates a synthetic spatiotemporal digital representation of livestock, wild-bird, and human systems for multihost H5N1 risk modeling.
The study develops and externally validates a survey and smart-card fusion framework whose reconstructed mobility flows materially alter epidemic estimates.
The study develops and evaluates a feature-selected multisource LSTM and moving-percentile threshold for influenza forecasting and early warning.
The paper specifies and demonstrates standardized data structures, processing procedures, and quality-control rules for a national infectious-disease wastewater-surveillance system.
The model predicts porcine body temperature for general livestock health, while cross-infection appears only as a drawback of rectal measurement rather than a modeled outcome.
The study develops and clinically evaluates a multimodal AI monitoring system that detects and immediately corrects surgical hand-hygiene errors relevant to infection prevention.
The review descriptively synthesizes plastisphere microbial assemblages and explicitly finds no confirmed pathogen transmission or direct human infectious risk.
The paper develops a structured reporting format for empirical evaluation of decision models and demonstrates it with an HPV and cervical-cancer microsimulation.
The review diagnoses weaknesses in HIV macroeconomic models and prescribes household-level data and microsimulation to represent long-term and distributional effects.

The study uses an individual-based HIV transmission model to project the population impact, cost-effectiveness, and implementation sensitivities of an RV144-like vaccine.
